response: "I’ll use the browser plus public web sources to verify VDOE’s actual FOIA rules/options, then I’ll draft and stage one request without submitting it."

action_line: websearch("\"objective\":\"Find Virginia Department of Education FOIA page, FOIA timelines, fee rules, exemptions, NextRequest portal\",\"search_queries\":[\"VDOE FOIA request\",\"Virginia Department Education FOIA\",\"VDOE NextRequest portal\"],\"mode\":\"advanced\"")
response: "{\"session_id\":\"qgPWbqFg8pBEooZN\",\"results\":[{\"source_id\":\"Tn7ogzfuxJDkkE9rplm3A\",\"url\":\"https://vaedu.nextrequest.com/\",\"title\":\"VA Freedom of Information Act (FOIA)NextRequest - Modern FOIA & Public Records Request Software \",\"publish_date\":null,\"excerpts\":[\"# VA Freedom of Information Act (FOIA)\\nThis web portal will help you communicate with your government about what documents you need.\\nMake Request\\nMake a new public records request.\\n[Help](https://www.civicplus.help/nextrequest/docs/submit-a-request-and-create-a-public-account)\\n[Privacy](https://www.nextrequest.com/privacypolicy)\\n[Terms](https://www.nextrequest.com/termsofservice)\\n[Learn more about FOIA requests](https://www.doe.virginia.gov/about-vdoe/foia-requests)\\n[](https://www.nextrequest.com)\"]},{\"source_id\":\"SkgTjMlBoUipxtujVtjKB\",\"url\":\"https://vadeq.nextrequest.com/\",\"title\":\"DEQ Freedom of Information Act Request NextRequest - Modern FOIA & Public Records Request Softwar...[truncated 15926 chars]...extrequest.com/\",\"title\":\"Public Records RequestsNextRequest - Modern FOIA & Public Records Request Software \",\"publish_date\":null,\"excerpts\":[\"# Public Records Requests\\nThis portal will help you communicate with the City of Mercer Island about what records you need. You can view previous requests and responsive documents. You may even find what you're looking for without having to submit a new request! The City’s online public records portal NextRequest is available 24-hours a day.\\n**Looking for Building Permit or Land Use Records?** Take advantage of the City's GIS site available **[here](https://chgis1.mercergov.org/Html5Viewer/Index.html?viewer=PubMaps&viewer=PubMaps)** for a convenient way to find what you are looking for. We also have a helpful **[Tip Sheet](https://www.mercerisland.gov/sites/default/files/fileattachments/community_planning_amp_development/page/2131/gis_records_search.pdf)** for detailed step-by-step instructions to assist you with using GIS.\"]}]}"

action_line: websearch("\"objective\":\"Find public Virginia Department of Education pages with records, reports, boards, data, grants, procurement, complaints, investigations likely relevant to FOIA\",\"search_queries\":[\"VDOE public records reports\",\"Virginia Department Education data reports\",\"VDOE grants procurement complaints\"],\"mode\":\"advanced\"")
response: "{\"session_id\":\"qgPWbqFg8pBEooZN\",\"results\":[{\"source_id\":\"l0nB7A6WBBqNhOZ7PWLJI\",\"url\":\"https://www.doe.virginia.gov/data-policy-funding/data-reports\",\"title\":\"Data & Reports | Virginia Department of Education\",\"publish_date\":null,\"excerpts\":[\"Commonwealth of Virginia branding bar cardinal logo\\n[Virginia Department of Education](https://www.doe.virginia.gov) An official website of the Commonwealth of Virginia Here's how you know An official website Here's how you know\\nServices Selection Icon Find a Commonwealth Resource Close Branding Bar Icon\\nSkip to page body Home Parents & Students Teaching, Learning & Assessment Programs & Services State Board, Data & Funding About VDOE\\nState Board, Data & Funding\\n\\n# Data & Reports\\nPrint\\nShare & Bookmark Share & Bookmark, Press Enter to show all options, press Tab go to next option\\n* Email\\n* Facebook\\n* LinkedIn\\n* Twitter\\n* Reddit\\nFont Size: + -\\nThe effective use of accurate and detailed informat...[truncated 19513 chars]...positive feedback** . Over the past two summers, the program has maintained a **50 percent waitlist** , with more than 30 students per level applying while only 15 could be enrolled. The grant funding directly addresses this capacity gap, ensuring more students can participate.\\nPost-camp survey data further supports the program’s effectiveness. Students report increased interest in STEM topics, stronger understanding of STEM career options, and greater confidence in applying problem-solving and computational thinking skills. Families frequently request additional STEM opportunities during the academic year, underscoring the camp’s impact as a catalyst for continued engagement.\\n\\n...\\n\\n### **Supporting Student Readiness and Career Awareness**\\nThrough this grant and the expansion of the STEM Exploration Camp, Colonial Heights Public Schools continues its commitment to providing innovative learning opportunities that prepare students for success in an ever-evolving world.\"]}]}"

action_line: websearch("\"objective\":\"Find VDOE public pages about school security equipment grants, awarded schools, criteria, applications, scoring\",\"search_queries\":[\"VDOE school security grants\",\"Virginia school security equipment grants\",\"VDOE security grant awards\"],\"mode\":\"advanced\"")
response: "{\"session_id\":\"qgPWbqFg8pBEooZN\",\"results\":[{\"source_id\":\"GxrjjiODKF1i9KsCc0Hcw\",\"url\":\"https://www.doe.virginia.gov/programs-services/school-operations-support-services/facility-construction-maintenance/security-equipment-grants\",\"title\":\"Security Equipment Grants - Virginia Department of Education\",\"publish_date\":null,\"excerpts\":[\"# Security Equipment Grants\\n## 2024-2025 Grants Awarded\\n* _News Release,_ November 21, 2024 _-_ [472 Virginia Schools Receive $12 Million in School Security Equipment Grants](https://www.doe.virginia.gov/Home/Components/News/News/437/227)\\n\\n...\\n\\n## Previous Grants Awarded\\n* _News Release,_ November 20, 2023 _-_ [483 Virginia Schools Receive $12 Million in School Security Equipment Grants](https://www.doe.virginia.gov/Home/Components/News/News/330/227)\\n* _News Release,_ November 10 _, 2022_ - [VDOE Announces $12 million in School Security Equipment Grants](https://www.doe.virginia.gov/Home/Components/News/News/83/227#!/...[truncated 23766 chars]...e/l0/https:%2f%2fwww.doe.virginia.gov%2fprograms-services%2fschool-operations-support-services%2fschool-facility-construction-maintenance%2fnew-school-safety-and-security-grant-fy24/3/01010190123e7bf8-1a4edd2d-2123-4912-9da6-f591b9285603-000000/ddqyjwpib4slapk-cywarxn8fsm=379\\\") . Detailed information was communicated to school divisions in [Virginia Education Update Article 2024-24-217](https://content.govdelivery.com/accounts/VADOE/bulletins/3a23f84) .\\n* The General Assembly approved $18.5 million for a new school safety and security grant for public schools from the general fund, titled the [FY 2024 School Safety and Security Grants](https://www.doe.virginia.gov/programs-services/school-operations-support-services/school-facility-construction-maintenance/new-school-safety-and-security-grant-fy24) . Detailed information was communicated to school divisions in [Virginia Education Update Article 2024-21-188](https://content.govdelivery.com/accounts/VADOE/bulletins/39e1ec8) .\"]}]}"

action_line: websearch("\"objective\":\"Find VDOE public pages about Virginia Literacy Act, division literacy plans, reading specialists, implementation, grants\",\"search_queries\":[\"VDOE Virginia Literacy Act\",\"Virginia literacy plans VDOE\",\"VDOE literacy grants implementation\"],\"mode\":\"advanced\"")
response: "{\"session_id\":\"qgPWbqFg8pBEooZN\",\"results\":[{\"source_id\":\"6Mt8-Cf9xefjEbhikcbvy\",\"url\":\"https://www.doe.virginia.gov/teaching-learning-assessment/k-12-standards-instruction/english-reading-literacy/literacy\",\"title\":\"Literacy | Virginia Department of Education\",\"publish_date\":null,\"excerpts\":[\"# Literacy\\n## Literacy Instruction\\nThe goal of literacy instruction in Virginia is to ensure that all children have the necessary skills to become successful readers, writers, speakers, and listeners with the critical thinking skills that are required to be successful as they progress and transition through the stages of their lives.\\n\\n### Virginia Literacy Act\\nWith the passage of the [Virginia Literacy Act (VLA)](https://www.doe.virginia.gov/teaching-learning-assessment/k-12-standards-instruction/english-reading-literacy/literacy/virginia-literacy-act) in the 2022 General Assembly, Virginia is taking the lead nationwide to improve early literacy outcomes for Virg...[truncated 25060 chars]...0)\\n* **April 29, 2025, Webinar [PowerPoint](https://www.doe.virginia.gov/home/showpublisheddocument/61515/638823921120370000) and [Recording](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fyoutu.be%2FXb8igEbn27w/1/01010196c59f9ee4-63a84352-dc95-4b5b-9911-25aa39ab3da0-000000/T3JggvFVc6-S7MYGIdGO71nrBv9vcHmomz0a5iF-_E8=404)** - The VDOE provided divisions with guidance for the revision of Division Literacy Plans for the biennial submission. The webinar provided participants with ideas of revision and strengthening of their Division Literacy Plans.\\n* **October 15, 2024, Webinar [PowerPoint](https://www.doe.virginia.gov/home/showpublisheddocument/57984/638646849144730000) and [Recording](https://youtu.be/hyEBIfNpq5U)** - The VDOE highlighted examples of strong components from Division Literacy Plans submitted July 1 during a webinar on October 15, 2024. This webinar supported divisions’ reflections on their own plans and helped divisions prepare for the December 6 resubmission.\"]}]}"

action_line: websearch("\"objective\":\"Find VDOE public pages about lab schools applications, awards, memoranda, contracts, performance reports\",\"search_queries\":[\"VDOE lab schools applications\",\"Virginia lab school grants\",\"VDOE college partnership laboratory schools\"],\"mode\":\"advanced\"")
response: "{\"session_id\":\"qgPWbqFg8pBEooZN\",\"results\":[{\"source_id\":\"ynYYEhjKUkTy7AvC-ZNeh\",\"url\":\"https://www.doe.virginia.gov/teaching-learning-assessment/specialized-instruction/laboratory-schools\",\"title\":\"Laboratory Schools | Virginia Department of Education\",\"publish_date\":null,\"excerpts\":[\"# Laboratory Schools\\n## Overview\\n### What Are College Partnership Laboratory Schools?\\nCollege Partnership Laboratory Schools—often called “Lab Schools”—are public, nonsectarian, and nonreligious schools in Virginia. They are public school programs created through partnerships with:\\n* Public institutions of higher education\\n* Public higher education centers, institutes, or authorities\\n* Eligible private colleges and universities (as defined by Virginia’s Tuition Assistance Grant Program\\nThese schools are designed to bring innovation directly into K–12 education by leveraging the expertise and resources of higher education institutions. A full list of eligible partners...[truncated 24452 chars]...rning: learning by doing,” CVCC President John Capps said. “It will provide dual-enrolled students with the opportunity to engage in hands-on problem-solving and skills development in high-demand CTE curricula. As a day-long program, the lab school will bring an experiential approach to other courses required for high school graduation so that traditional subjects like English and government will reinforce students’ technical classes.”\\nTo date, VDOE has awarded 15 college laboratory planning grants totaling more than $2.8 million.\\nThe 2022 General Assembly appropriated $100 million to the commonwealth’s College Partnership Laboratory School Fund, with up to $5 million designated for planning grants to colleges and universities to develop plans for new lab schools. More information about college lab schools is available [on the VDOE website](https://www.doe.virginia.gov/teaching-learning-assessment/specialized-instruction/laboratory-schools) .\\n# # #\\nReturn to full list >>\"]}]}"

action_line: websearch("\"objective\":\"Find VDOE public pages about special education complaints dispute resolution, findings letters, data reports\",\"search_queries\":[\"VDOE special education complaints\",\"Virginia special education dispute resolution\",\"VDOE due process complaints\"],\"mode\":\"advanced\"")
response: "{\"session_id\":\"qgPWbqFg8pBEooZN\",\"results\":[{\"source_id\":\"xYpkNmbABY9kMt75ZWKA5\",\"url\":\"https://www.doe.virginia.gov/programs-services/special-education/resolving-disputes/resolving-disputes\",\"title\":\"Special Education Complaints | Virginia Department of Education\",\"publish_date\":null,\"excerpts\":[\"Commonwealth of Virginia branding bar cardinal logo\\n[Virginia Department of Education](https://www.doe.virginia.gov) An official website of the Commonwealth of Virginia Here's how you know An official website Here's how you know\\nServices Selection Icon Find a Commonwealth Resource Close Branding Bar Icon\\nLoading ...\"]},{\"source_id\":\"kdzbyulSawWHBFyPwQXUt\",\"url\":\"https://www.fcps.edu/academics/curriculum/special-education/procedural-support/dispute-resolution-options\",\"title\":\"Dispute Resolution Options | Fairfax County Public Schools\",\"publish_date\":null,\"excerpts\":[\"# # Dispute Resolution Options\\n## Administrative Review\\nThe entire process ...[truncated 25698 chars]...f eligibility\\n• Change in IEP\\n• Change in placement or preventing a change\\nin placement\\n• Reversal of manifestation determination\\ndecision\\n• Retrospective Relief\\n• Compensatory education\\n• Tuition reimbursement\\n• Attorney fees + costs ( 34 CFR §300.517(a)(1)(i))\\n• NO expert witness fees\\n• NO punitive damages\\n• **Section 504/ADA**\\n• Retrospective Relief\\n• Compensatory education\\n• Tuition reimbursement\\n• Monetary/compensatory damages\\n• Intentional discrimination\\n• e.g., bad faith/gross misjudgment\\n• Attorney fees + costs\\n• Expert witness fees\\n• NO punitive damages\\nNOTE: In both, school can recover litigation costs\\nfrom parents when the underlying action is frivolous,\\nunreasonable, or without foundation\\n**VDOE Guidance**\\n**Documents**\\nOTHER OPTIONS\\n• Ombudsman for Special Education – an informal source of\\ninformation and referral, aids in answering individuals’ questions and\\nassists in the resolution of concerns and issues\"]}]}"

action_line: websearch("\"objective\":\"Find VDOE public pages about school accreditation support framework and division/school support plans, corrective actions, monitoring\",\"search_queries\":[\"VDOE accreditation support framework\",\"Virginia school support plans VDOE\",\"VDOE academic review schools\"],\"mode\":\"advanced\"")
response: "{\"session_id\":\"qgPWbqFg8pBEooZN\",\"results\":[{\"source_id\":\"FN6da6LzeCqURgSKUh-5b\",\"url\":\"https://www.doe.virginia.gov/programs-services/school-operations-support-services/school-quality/needs-assessments-and-support-plans\",\"title\":\"\\n\\t\\n    Needs Assessments and Support Plans | Virginia Department of Education\\n\\n\",\"publish_date\":null,\"excerpts\":[\"- School Accreditation\\n+ Accountability\\n- Federal Accountability\\n+ School Quality Profiles\\n+ Exemplar Awards\\n+ Data & Reports\\n- Interactive Data Dashboards\\n- Statistics & Reports\\n- Program Participation Data\\n- Data Collections\\n- Virginia Longitudinal Data System (VLDS)\\\")\\n- Virginia’s Visualization and Analytics Solution (VVAAS)\\\")\\n- Learning Analytics to Support Education and Reporting (LASER)\\\")\\n+ School Finance\\n- Budget & Grants Management\\n- Educational Technology Notes\\n- School Division Efficiency Reviews\\n- Procurement\\n- Travel Reimbursement\\n- Neighborhood Assistance...[truncated 21666 chars]...{\"source_id\":\"_7e_CY2I6lSCQnXOb1mpV\",\"url\":\"https://www.rcps.info/students-families/policies-procedures-handbooks/long-range-plans\",\"title\":\"Comprehensive School Support Plans (CSSPs) - Roanoke City Public Schools\",\"publish_date\":null,\"excerpts\":[\"# Comprehensive School Support Plans (CSSPs)\\nVirginia law requires each school division to develop a long-range, comprehensive plan that is based on data and focused on improving classroom instruction and student achievement. These plans are created with input from staff and the community, reviewed every two years, and updated as needed. School divisions must also make the plans available to the public online or in print and hold a public hearing before adopting them.\\nView your school's plan and the Roanoke City Public Schools division-wide plan below:\\n\\n## extract-horizontal-rule\\nDO NOT REMOVE !! This is logo for decorative horizontal rule. You can change this on location settings.\\nMission statement graphic\"]}]}"

action_line: websearch("\"objective\":\"Find VDOE January 7 2026 CLSD literacy grants news release and award details\",\"search_queries\":[\"VDOE Millions Invested literacy 2026\",\"Virginia literacy grant January 2026\",\"Comprehensive Literacy State Development VDOE January\"],\"mode\":\"advanced\"")
response: "{\"session_id\":\"qgPWbqFg8pBEooZN\",\"results\":[{\"source_id\":\"h6Qj3n5aXx-41hQXPbkkL\",\"url\":\"https://www.doe.virginia.gov/Home/Components/News/News/514/227\",\"title\":\"Millions Invested in Virginia to Help Students Learn to Read and ...\",\"publish_date\":null,\"excerpts\":[\"# News Releases\\n## Millions Invested in Virginia to Help Students Learn to Read and Build Literacy Skills\\nPost Date: 01/07/2026 4:10 PM\\nFor Immediate Release: January 7, 2025 **\\n** Contact: communications@doe.virginia.gov\\n**Richmond, VA** -- Virginia continues to expand its effort to help children learn to read and build literacy skills. The Virginia Department of Education has named Year 2 recipients of the Comprehensive Literacy State Development (CLSD) Grant, a five-year, $30 million award from the U.S. Department of Education. The grant focuses on improving reading instruction for students and schools with the greatest needs, from early childhood to high school.\\nIn its second year, the g...[truncated 24436 chars]... [Literacy](https://languagemagazine.com/category/language-news/news/literacy/ \\\"View all posts in Literacy\\\") US Department of Ed Awards $256 Million in Literacy Grants\\n\\n...\\n\\n# US Department of Ed Awards $256 Million in Literacy Grants\\n[](https://ncte.org/text-and-tech/?utm_source=partner&utm_medium=website&utm_campaign=sora2026)\\nThe US Department of Education has announced $256 million in new Education Innovation and Research (EIR) grants to improve literacy nationwide. These grants are the first awards made using three of Education Secretary McMahon’s grant-making priorities: _strengthening evidence-based literacy instruction_ , _expanding education choice_ , and _returning education to the states_ .\\nAs a result of the secretary’s policy of “returning education to the states,” ten of the 24 new awards went to state education agencies—marking the largest number of state-led literacy awards in any EIR competition and a significant increase over previous years.\"]}]}"
